title: The Science of Sleep
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Sleep is not simply a (0) scientific curiosity – it is a biological necessity. Modern research confirms that getting enough rest is truly (17) essential for both physical and mental wellbeing. Without adequate sleep, people suffer from extreme (18) tiredness that can make even simple tasks difficult. Poor sleep also reduces the (19) concentration needed to study or work effectively. Regular, restful sleep helps maintain a (20) healthy immune system and reduces the risk of many serious diseases. The body follows an internal (21) regulation system known as the circadian rhythm, which controls when we feel sleepy or alert. (22) Consequently, when this rhythm is disrupted by late nights or travel, people often feel confused and unwell. Research has shown that even one (23) insufficient night of sleep can affect memory, judgement, and reaction time. Long-term sleep deprivation can lead to serious medical conditions and (24) dysfunction of normal mental processes.

example.text: Sleep is not simply a (0) SCIENTIFIC curiosity – it is a biological necessity. (SCIENCE)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed after 'truly'; 'essence' becomes 'essential' by changing '-ce' to '-tial'.
A noun is needed after 'extreme'; 'tire' → 'tired', then 'tiredness' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
A noun is needed after 'the'; 'concentrate' becomes 'concentration' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
An adjective is needed before 'immune system'; 'health' becomes 'healthy' by adding the suffix '-y'.
A noun is needed before 'system'; 'regulate' becomes 'regulation' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
An adverb is needed at the start of the clause; 'consequence' → 'consequent' → 'consequently' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'in-' is added to 'sufficient' to mean 'not enough'.
A negative noun is needed; the prefix 'dys-' is added to 'function' to mean 'impaired or abnormal functioning'.
